assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 42-64 contain the full Security Configuration section with all three subsections matching the reference file exactly: Product Lifecycle (lines 44-50 with all 5 fields: Product pages URL, Jira version prefix, Vulnerability issue type ID, Component label pattern, VEX Justification custom field), Version Streams (lines 52-56 with 2.1.x stream row), and Source Repositories (lines 58-64 with backend and frontend-ui rows). All values match the reference file character-for-character.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 83-90) states: 'Security Configuration exists with all required fields populated and no {{placeholder}} markers' and concludes 'Result: Security Configuration is up to date.' assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed

Line 23 also states: 'All existing sections were preserved exactly as found. No content was added, removed, or overwritten.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 83-90) shows the skill detected the existing Security Configuration with all fields populated and no placeholders, resulting in 'Security Configuration is up to date.' There is no mention of an opt-in prompt being offered in any of the output files. The changes-log.md confirms the section was preserved with no changes needed. The skill recognized the section was complete and skipped it.
